author: lOrm
content: Fixed at HEAD (working tree on main, pending commit). src/thinking.rs::has_ultrathink_keyword now scans the raw &str.as_bytes() with allocation-free ASCII-case-insensitive helpers (find_whole_word_ci + contains_ci + matches_ci_at) and no longer calls text.to_lowercase(). ULTRATHINK_PHRASES stored as &[&[u8]] of pre-lowered bytes; ULTRATHINK_NEEDLE constant for the whole-word case. Word-boundary semantics preserved (case bytes are lowered point-wise via to_ascii_lowercase on the haystack side only). New regression test thinking::tests::large_input_case_insensitive_match exercises a 256 KiB buffer with positive ("UltraThink"), negative (no trigger), embedded ("myUltraThinker" must NOT match), and multi-word ("THINK ULTRA HARD") cases. cargo test --lib thinking:: passes 7/7. cargo clippy --lib --all-features -- -D warnings is clean.
